title: Avoid Using Octal Values in Java
description: In Java, integer literals starting with a zero are interpreted as octal values. This can be confusing for developers without experience with octal values, who may misinterpret them as decimal values. It is recommended to avoid using octal values to prevent confusion and potential errors.
recommendation: Replace octal values with their decimal equivalents. For example, replace '010' with '8'.
impact: Using octal values can lead to misunderstanding and incorrect interpretation of the code, affecting its quality and potentially leading to errors.

noncompliant: int myNumber = 010; // Noncompliant. myNumber will hold 8, not 10 - was this really expected?

checks.message: Replace octal values with their decimal equivalents.
